title: Cracca lurida
taxonomy: Cracca lurida belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Cracca. The genus Cracca is a group of plants closely related to the genus Robinia. Cracca lurida is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is often classified as a shrub or small tree.
uses: Cracca lurida, also known as the Yellow Pea, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential benefits in treating various health conditions. Its roots and leaves have been employed to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant contains alkaloids and glycosides that may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use. Further studies are required to fully understand the potential benefits and risks of using Cracca lurida as a medicinal plant.

parts: Cracca lurida is a plant with various traditional uses, and its medicinal parts are primarily its root and leaves.
